questions.q: Within the Union government, the Prime Minister is described as the head of which branch?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is the head of government and leader of the Council of Ministers, holding the real executive authority of the Union.

questions.q: Who formally appoints the Prime Minister of India?
options: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: Under the Constitution, the Prime Minister is appointed by the President of India.

questions.q: The Prime Minister is usually the leader of the majority in which House of Parliament?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister is usually the leader of the party or coalition commanding a majority in the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru served as the first Prime Minister of India from 1947.

questions.q: The Prime Minister heads which body that advises the President in executive functions?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister leads the Council of Ministers, which aids and advises the President.

questions.q: Who was the first woman to serve as Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Indira Gandhi was the first and, to date, only woman to serve as Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: The Prime Minister and the Council of Ministers are collectively responsible to which House?
questions.explanation: Under the principle of collective responsibility, the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister is responsible to the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which of these is a key role of the Prime Minister in relation to the Council of Ministers?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister recommends the names of ministers to the President, who then appoints them.

questions.q: Who was the youngest person to become Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Rajiv Gandhi became Prime Minister in 1984 at the age of 40, the youngest to hold the office.

questions.q: The official residence of the Prime Minister of India is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister's official residence is in New Delhi, the national capital.

questions.q: Which article of the Constitution provides for the appointment of the Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Article 75 states that the Prime Minister shall be appointed by the President and other ministers appointed on the Prime Minister's advice.

questions.q: Who was the first Prime Minister of India who did not belong to the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: Morarji Desai became Prime Minister in 1977 as head of the Janata Party, the first not from the Congress.

questions.q: Article 78 of the Constitution chiefly deals with which duty of the Prime Minister?
options: Communicating Council of Ministers' decisions to the President
questions.explanation: Article 78 makes it the duty of the Prime Minister to communicate decisions of the Council of Ministers to the President.

questions.q: Who served as the second Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Lal Bahadur Shastri served as the second Prime Minister of India from 1964 to 1966.

questions.q: Who was the first Sikh to hold the office of Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Manmohan Singh, who served from 2004 to 2014, was the first Sikh to be Prime Minister.

questions.q: A person can be appointed Prime Minister even without being a member of either House, provided they become a member within how many months?
questions.explanation: A non-member appointed as a minister, including the Prime Minister, must become a member of either House within six months.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister was the first from South India to complete a full five-year term?
questions.explanation: P.V. Narasimha Rao, Prime Minister from 1991 to 1996, was the first from South India to serve a full term.

questions.q: Who has been the longest-serving Prime Minister of India?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru served continuously from 1947 to 1964, the longest tenure as Prime Minister.

questions.q: Atal Bihari Vajpayee holds which distinction regarding the office of Prime Minister?
options: First Prime Minister from South India
First non-Congress Prime Minister to complete a full term
questions.explanation: Vajpayee was the first non-Congress Prime Minister to complete a full term, from 1999 to 2004.

questions.q: If the Prime Minister is a member of the Rajya Sabha, which is constitutionally permissible, the government must still retain the confidence of which House?
questions.explanation: Regardless of which House the Prime Minister belongs to, the government must command the confidence of the Lok Sabha.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister served from 1989 to 1990 at the head of the National Front coalition?
questions.explanation: Vishwanath Pratap Singh served as Prime Minister from 1989 to 1990 leading the National Front.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister coined the slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan'?
questions.explanation: Lal Bahadur Shastri coined the slogan 'Jai Jawan Jai Kisan' during his tenure.

questions.q: Who served as acting Prime Minister of India on two occasions following the deaths of serving Prime Ministers in the 1960s?
questions.explanation: Gulzarilal Nanda served as acting Prime Minister after the deaths of Nehru in 1964 and Shastri in 1966.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister served a brief tenure from July 1979 to January 1980, among the shortest for an elected Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: Charan Singh served as Prime Minister from July 1979 to January 1980, among the shortest tenures.

questions.q: The Constitution does not fix a separate term for the Prime Minister; the office continues so long as the Prime Minister enjoys what?
options: The confidence of the Lok Sabha
questions.explanation: The Prime Minister holds office as long as the Council of Ministers retains the confidence of the Lok Sabha, with no separate fixed term.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister served three separate terms, including a brief first term in 1996 and a later full term?
questions.explanation: Atal Bihari Vajpayee served three terms as Prime Minister, including a brief term in 1996 and a full term from 1999 to 2004.

questions.q: Under Article 74, the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister aids and advises the President, who is bound to act in accordance with that advice following which amendment?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment (1976) made the President's exercise of functions explicitly subject to the advice of the Council of Ministers under Article 74.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister served from 1997 to 1998 and is associated with a doctrine on relations with neighbouring countries?
questions.explanation: Inder Kumar Gujral served as Prime Minister from 1997 to 1998 and is associated with the 'Gujral Doctrine'.

questions.q: Constitutionally, what is the salary and allowances of the Prime Minister determined by?
questions.explanation: The salary and allowances of ministers, including the Prime Minister, are determined by Parliament by law as provided under Article 75.

questions.q: Which Prime Minister led the United Front coalition government from 1996 to 1997 and had earlier been Chief Minister of Karnataka?
questions.explanation: H.D. Deve Gowda served as Prime Minister from 1996 to 1997 and had earlier been Chief Minister of Karnataka.
